memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const float *x, const float *gamma, const void *W_q4k, int M, int K, float eps)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm_linear_q4k

detaileddescription.para: Computes: y = Linear(RMSNorm(x)) where Linear uses Q4_K weights and Q8_K activations internally.
The key optimization is that the normalized values never touch DRAM - they go directly from RMSNorm computation to Q8_K quantization to GEMV.

parameterdescription.para: Linear weights in Q4_K format, shape [M, K]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const float *x, const float *gamma, const void *W_q4k, int M, int K, float eps)
memberdef.name: unfused_rmsnorm_linear_q4k_ref

detaileddescription.para: This is the SLOW version that does separate RMSNorm and GEMV calls, with intermediate results going to DRAM.

sect1.title: FUSION BENEFIT:
sect1.para: Unfused: RMSNorm(x) → [DRAM write: norm_out] → Quantize → [DRAM write: q8] → GEMV Total DRAM: 2 writes + 2 reads = 4 * hidden_size bytes
Fused: RMSNorm(x) → [registers] → Quantize → [stack/L1: q8] → GEMV Total DRAM: 0 intermediate writes/reads
Expected: 2-4x memory traffic reduction for this operation
